Cult Manifestations - Tzeentch.result: The lumen-globes in the corridor flicker in a rhythmic pattern — one observant character might notice it matches the cadence of the Seraph preachers' chanting.
A reflection in a puddle of acid-runoff shows the party from an angle that doesn't match their actual position. It corrects itself when directly observed.
A sudden gust of hot, incense-scented air from a ventilation shaft. The temperature spikes unnaturally, then snaps back to the hive's usual chill. Something smells like burning feathers.
The party passes graffiti of an aquila on a wall. One head of the eagle appears to have nine eyes instead of two. Looking again, it's normal — but was it always?
A distant sound of choral singing, beautiful and harmonious, drifts from a direction where no cathedrum or chapel exists. It stops the moment anyone tries to locate the source.

A character's data-slate briefly displays a string of unintelligible symbols before returning to normal. A Challenging (+0) Linguistics Test identifies fragments of pre-Imperial cipher — the same type Halikarn used in his scroll.
A local rat scurries past with iridescent, colour-shifting fur. It pauses, seems to regard the party with unsettling intelligence, then vanishes into a crack in the rockrete.
Reeta von Mard suddenly gasps and grips her head — she senses a psychic 'echo', like a conversation happening just beyond the edge of perception. She can make out a single word: a character's name.

The shadows in this corridor are wrong. They bend at angles that don't match the light source and seem to point towards the Cathedrum district. A Routine (+20) Awareness (Sight) Test confirms they are genuinely moving.
